content:
AUDIENCE:  
The reader is a senior business executive with a demanding schedule, used to high-level decision-making rather than detailed technical explanations. They have a strong grasp of financial concepts in general but no deep background in mathematics or science. This person values clear, concise information with practical relevance to business or personal finance. They likely understand basic interest but may not fully appreciate the exponential power of compound interest. They want straightforward, jargon-free explanations that highlight benefits and risks without unnecessary detail. Overly technical or abstract explanations would bore or confuse them. A confident, professional tone with real-world analogies or examples will resonate best, emphasizing how compound interest can significantly grow savings over time.

===BEGIN===  
Compound interest is a powerful tool that helps your savings grow faster than simple interest. Unlike simple interest, which earns a fixed percentage only on the initial amount, compound interest earns returns on both your original savings and the accumulated interest. Think of it as “interest on interest.” Over time, this means your money doesn’t just grow steadily—it accelerates. For example, if you invest $10,000 at a 5% annual compound interest rate, after one year you earn $500 in interest. The next year, you earn interest on $10,500, and this continues to build. The longer you leave your money to grow, the more dramatic this effect becomes, turning modest savings into substantial wealth. For busy executives like you, understanding and leveraging compound interest can be a simple yet effective way to maximize the value of your investments without adding complexity to your financial planning.  
===END===
